name: Singin' Hinney
recipeInstructions:
1. Lightly grease a large thick-based frying pan or griddle.
2. Sift together the flour, bicarbonate of soda, cream of tartar, and salt in a bowl.
3. Add the lard, cut into small pieces, and rub it into the dry ingredients with your fingertips until the mixture resembles fine breadcrumbs.
4. Gradually add currants and about 6 tablespoons of milk; stir with a fork until the dough forms a soft texture.
5. Place the frying pan over low heat. Turn the dough out onto a floured surface and knead lightly.
6. Roll the dough into a circle, approximately 8 inches in diameter.
7. Place the rolled-out pastry in the hot frying pan and cook until golden brown on the underside, about 15 minutes.
8. Using a fish slice, carefully turn the pastry over and press down the edges with the slice. Cook on the other side for an additional 8 to 10 minutes.
9. Remove from the pan, split the flatbread in half with a sharp knife, spread each half with butter, sandwich together, and cut into wedges.
10. Serve hot.
